title: Top 12 Custom Athletic Clothing Manufacturers In Usa
body_html:
Finding the right custom gym wear manufacturer in the USA is harder than it sounds. Dozens of suppliers claim to be the best — but most fall short on quality, minimums, or turnaround time.

You might be launching a fitness brand from scratch. Maybe you're scaling a private label line, or pulling your supply chain back stateside. Either way, the manufacturer you pick will directly shape your margins, your timeline, and your brand reputation.

So we did the research for you. We vetted and ranked the top 12 custom athletic clothing manufacturers in the USA — so you can skip the guesswork and get straight to building.

This list covers everything from low-MOQ startups to full-package cut-and-sew operations. There's a match for every budget and business model.

1. Tack Apparel — Best for Full-Package Custom Athletic Wear Development

Tack Apparel does what most domestic manufacturers won't. They take you from a rough sketch all the way to bulk production — under one roof.

Based in Los Angeles, CA, Tack Apparel is built for emerging fitness brands that need real support through the production process. Not just a factory that runs with your finished tech pack. As a full-service private label athletic clothing manufacturer , they handle fabric sourcing, spec development, sampling, custom branding, and bulk production — all in one workflow.

What makes them stand out for athletic wear brands:

  • Low MOQ structure — around 50–100 pieces per style. This makes them a solid fit for brands still testing demand

  • Concept-to-bulk support — no tech pack? They'll build one from your sketches

  • Custom branding execution — woven labels, embroidered logos, hangtags, and branded packaging are all included

  • Performance fabric guidance — they point you toward the right polyester-spandex or nylon-spandex blends based on end-use (gym tops vs. compression leggings)

Core categories covered: gym wear sets, leggings, sports bras, hoodies, teamwear, and accessories like socks. You can build a multi-SKU brand without managing multiple suppliers.

⚠️ One caveat: There are isolated reports online of payment disputes with no product delivered. Protect yourself — use staged payments, get everything in writing, and start with a sample order before committing to bulk.

Contact: tackapparel.com | sales@tackapparel.com | +1 646-681-4212

2. Affix Apparel (Los Angeles, CA) — Best Low MOQ Custom Activewear Manufacturer USA

Fifty pieces. That's Affix Apparel's entry point — and for a startup testing its first fitness line, that number is hard to beat.

Also based in Los Angeles, Affix Apparel has spent about 10 years building a private-label and made-to-order operation. Today, they serve over 2,000 clients — from small startups to large-scale brands. Their model is simple: concept, production, and shipment all handled under one roof.

What makes Affix worth considering:

  • ~50-piece MOQ — low enough for early-stage brands to test ideas before scaling up

  • Full-service workflow — design, manufacturing, and shipping through one activewear vendor. No juggling multiple contacts

  • Private-label branding — you build a distinct brand identity from day one

  • 10 years of manufacturing experience — this is an established operation, not a newcomer

⚠️ Do your due diligence: Public sources don't confirm a dedicated activewear-only line. Before you order, get written confirmation of any Made in USA claim eligibility. Also request the factory address and ask for compliance documents or client QC records.

Contact: affixapparel.com | sales@affixapparel.com | (323) 713-0672

3. Zega Apparel (Wyoming, USA / Pakistan Production) — Best for Budget-Conscious Startups

Here's the math that matters: 300 pieces across 3 core styles, total production budget around $3,000–$4,500 . That's what Zega Apparel's Pakistan-based production can deliver — a number that's almost impossible to hit with a US-only activewear manufacturer.

Zega runs a hybrid model. The company is registered in Sheridan, Wyoming . That gives you a US contract entity, English-speaking support, and clean invoicing. The actual manufacturing happens in Karachi, Pakistan — where labor and fabric costs run 20–40% lower than comparable US cut-and-sew operations.

They bring 10+ years of factory experience and have worked with 3,000+ brand clients . This is not a new operation taking risks with your order.

What works for early-stage fitness brands:

  • MOQ as low as 50–100 pieces per style — a solid starting point for a first drop

  • Private label branding included — custom hang tags, woven labels, and logo embroidery add around $0.20–$0.50 per piece

  • Concept-to-delivery support — no tech pack? Their team helps build one from scratch

⚠️ Critical caveat: Products are made in Pakistan — not the USA. Your brand positioning requires a "Made in USA" label? This manufacturer is not your match. Plan your budget to cover import duties. Also, add extra lead time for international shipping delays.

Contact: zegaapparel.com

4. Texasia (Houston, TX) — Best Sustainable Custom Sportswear Manufacturer USA

Sustainability isn't a buzzword anymore — it's a buying decision. Texasia is built around that reality.

Texasia operates as a sustainable custom sportswear OEM/ODM manufacturer . They hold both BSCI and Sedex certifications — two of the most recognized credentials in ethical supply chain manufacturing. Fitness brands selling eco-conscious activewear will find these certifications carry real weight with today's buyers.

What Texasia brings to sustainable sportswear production:

  • OEM and ODM services — bring a finished design or just a rough concept

  • Eco-friendly materials — stated in their brand positioning

  • Custom production — built for brand-specific sportswear lines

⚠️ Due diligence required: The factory is Bangladesh-based, not Houston. Exact MOQ, lead times, and specific recycled fabric types (rPET, recycled nylon) are not confirmed on their site . Before you commit, ask for fabric composition sheets, BSCI/Sedex certificate numbers, and written proof of any sustainability label compliance.

Contact: texasia.com

5. Mega Sports Apparel (Los Angeles, CA) — Best Team Uniforms & Custom Sublimation Printing

Full-panel, seam-to-seam color. No color count limits. No per-color setup fees. That's the core advantage of sublimation printing. Mega Sports Apparel built their whole operation around it.

They specialize in cut & sew, sublimation, embroidery, and printing for sportswear: tees, shorts, leggings, sports bras, and full sets. Their sweet spot is team uniforms and branded gym apparel where all-over print matters.

What works here:

  • 50-piece MOQ — fits small clubs, gym merch launches, or corporate event runs

  • Per-player name and number customization — each player's details merge into individual print files, no extra tooling cost

  • All-over sublimation — gradients, camo, multi-color logos, and fades all come at the same price

  • Design and fabric sourcing support — a real plus if you're starting without a finished tech pack

⚠️ Use caution: At least one Reddit thread labels them a scam. Reviews.io shows positive feedback on leggings and print quality. Protect yourself — start with a paid sample. Confirm their physical address. Use staged payments.

Contact: megasportsapparel.com | info@megasportsapparel.com | +1 (805) 468-8262

6. Precision Sportswear (Portland, OR) — Best for Performance-Focused Custom Athletic Clothing

Portland isn't just home to Nike and Adidas. It's a full performance-apparel ecosystem — and Precision Sportswear is right at the center of it.

Their own words say it best: "cutting-edge technology combined with innovative creativity to create high quality clothing that cannot be matched." That's not casual athleisure talk. That's a performance-first manufacturer speaking.

What makes them a strong fit for technical athletic brands:

  • Performance fabric depth — You get compression knits (65–80% polyester/nylon + 20–35% elastane), moisture-wicking blends, UPF 30–50+ outdoor fabrics, and flatlock seam construction. That last detail matters — flatlock seams cut down on chafing during high-movement activity.

  • Fast domestic lead times — Team uniforms ship within 4–8 weeks. Offshore orders take 8–16 weeks. That's a real difference for tight schedules.

  • Low MOQs — Orders start at just 24–50 units per style. This works well for club orders or seasonal drops.

  • Local sourcing advantage — Precision is close to Portland's Functional Fabric Fair. That gives them early access to next-generation compression knits and warp-knit performance textiles — ahead of most other manufacturers.

Best fit: Premium performance brands, serious amateur teams, and endurance-focused labels. Wholesale price benchmarks run $35–$60 for team jerseys and $30–$55 for compression tights. Yes, that's higher than mass athleisure — but the technical specs back it up.

⚠️ Note: MOQ and pricing details aren't confirmed on their site. Reach out with your performance spec sheet before you commit.

Contact: precisionsportswear.com

7. Albek Sportswear (Los Angeles, CA) — Reliable USA-Based Sportswear Manufacturer for Growing Brands

Leggings and sports bras aren't generic products. They need precise stretch recovery, opacity, and seam durability. Albek Sportswear in Los Angeles is built around exactly those categories.

Their focus is clear: this is a strong starting point for brands launching custom yoga wear, fitted leggings, and sports bra lines . Your brand is in the stretch-knit and compression activewear space? That deep specialization beats a factory that does everything at an average level.

Before you commit, ask these questions:

  • Sample lead time and bulk lead time

  • MOQ per style and colorway

  • Fabric GSM (target: 200–300 gsm stretch knit), spandex content ( 10–25% ), and construction method (flatlock or coverstitch)

  • Squat-proof and wash-test results

⚠️ Gap to flag: Public data doesn't confirm Albek's MOQ, certifications, or large-volume capacity. Start with samples. Build the relationship on production evidence, not promises.

Contact: albeksportswear.com

8. Tri-Star Manufacturing (Pittsburgh, PA) — Established Contract Manufacturer for Athletic Apparel

Pittsburgh isn't the first city you'd think of for athletic apparel. That gap is what makes it worth a closer look from a supply chain angle.

Tri-Star Manufacturing is a contract manufacturer built for brands that are ready to scale. You come in with a complete tech pack, graded specs, and a solid forecast. They take it from there and produce at volume.

This is not a startup-friendly shop. They work with brands running 300–500+ pieces per style , with annual volume in the tens of thousands of units. Still testing a concept? Look elsewhere on this list.

Why the Pittsburgh location works in your favor:

  • 1 day ground to Philadelphia and D.C.

  • 1–2 days to New York/New Jersey

  • 2–3 days to Boston and Atlanta

That's 3–7 days domestic ex-factory versus 30–45 days from Asia. For replenishment programs and tight seasonal windows, that gap is a real advantage.

⚠️ Verify before you commit: Public records don't confirm a Pittsburgh-based "Tri-Star" operating in athletic apparel. Before reaching out, ask for their factory address, client references, and AQL documentation.

Contact: Reach out to confirm current capabilities and MOQ requirements.

9. Apparel Production Inc. (New York, NY) — Full-Service Activewear Manufacturer for NYC-Based Brands

Seventy-five years in the Garment District. That's not a marketing claim — that's a track record.

Founded in 1947, Apparel Production Inc. operates out of 270 W 39th St — the beating heart of New York's fashion manufacturing scene. Fabric suppliers, trim houses, and sample rooms sit within walking distance. For NYC-based activewear brands, that closeness cuts development time by days, not weeks. Fabric sourcing that takes weeks elsewhere? Done in one to two days here.

What makes them work for activewear brands:

  • End-to-end production — design, pattern making, sampling, QC, and private label delivery all under one roof

  • Fast domestic turnaround — basic activewear samples (leggings, tees, joggers) in 7–10 business days

  • Low entry MOQ — 30–100 pieces per style, in line with standard Garment District norms

  • Face-to-face fit sessions — walk in, try samples on, fix patterns in real time

Their background covers uniforms and functional apparel — police gear, medical gowns, structured outerwear. That experience carries over into activewear production. You get wash durability, solid stretch recovery, and precise sizing built into every run.

⚠️ Caveat: Activewear isn't their sole focus. Confirm fabric specs, MOQ, and lead times with them before committing.

Contact: (212) 278-8362 | 270 W 39th St, 17th Floor, New York, NY 10018

10. Los Angeles Apparel — Best USA-Made Blanks for Custom Athletic Private Label

Not every brand needs a full cut-and-sew operation. Sometimes the fastest path to market is a premium blank with your logo on it. LA Apparel is the USA-made blanks supplier built for that.

Founded by Dov Charney, LA Apparel runs out of South Central Los Angeles. That "Made in South Central" identity isn't just a tagline. It's a real ESG and origin story your brand can build on.

The workflow is simple:

  • Pick your blank style (tees, fleece, hoodies, joggers)

  • Set your quantity

  • Submit artwork for screen print, DTG, embroidery, patches, heat transfer, or foil printing

Their flagship blanks — like the 1801GD (6.5 oz, garment-dyed, 18-singles cotton) — give you a heavy, quality handfeel. That puts your private-label gym merch and athleisure pieces above mass-market options.

The honest limitation: this isn't cut-and-sew. You can't build performance panels, custom stretch zones, or unique silhouettes. Your brand needs technical activewear? Look elsewhere on this list.

For USA-made private label gym tops, branded crewnecks, and drop-ready merch collections , LA Apparel is a fast, solid shortcut.

Contact: losangelesapparel.net

11. Royal Apparel — Best Eco-Friendly Activewear Basics Manufacturer in USA

Royal Apparel has been knitting sustainable blanks in the USA since 1993 — long before "eco-friendly" became a marketing buzzword.

Their eco program covers a wide range of materials: 100% certified organic cotton , organic viscose bamboo , organic hemp blends , and recycled polyester/organic cotton blends . That last option is worth noting. A 50/50 or 60/40 mix at 140–170 gsm hits the sweet spot for performance tees. You get moisture-wicking fabric without giving up your sustainability story.

What to specify when you reach out:

  • Lightweight eco jersey (140–160 gsm) for training tees and tanks

  • Organic fleece (250–300 gsm) for hoodies and joggers

  • Custom Pantone colors — plan for 300–600 pcs per color/style as a baseline MOQ

Good On You rates their environmental performance as "good" . That's a real third-party signal for brands targeting Gen Z buyers. Over 60% of that group prefer eco-made activewear and factor it into their buying decisions.

The real advantage here isn't just the fabrics. It's the full package. Domestic production + certified organic fibers + shorter supply chains — this combination gives your brand a clear, honest sustainability story. Imported basics can't match that.

⚠️ Due diligence note: Royal does not confirm GOTS or OEKO-TEX certifications on their main eco pages. Before ordering, request mill certificates and transaction certificates for any certified organic claims.

Contact: royalapparel.net

12. Lefty Production Co. (Los Angeles, CA) — Best for Activewear & Athleisure Startup Brands

Twelve years in Downtown LA's Fashion District. A team of 51. $5.4 million in annual revenue. Lefty Production Co. isn't a side project. It's a real development house built for activewear and athleisure startup brands.

Their model covers everything in one place: concept, pattern-making, fabric sourcing, cutting, sewing, finishing, and fulfillment. No tech pack? No problem. They'll take your idea and turn it into a finished collection from scratch.

Why startup activewear brands choose Lefty:

  • Activewear + athleisure specialization — not a generic cut-and-sew shop. Their entire focus is performance wear and everyday athletic styles. That's all they do.

  • Concept-to-collection workflow — a solid fit for founders with no in-house pattern makers or fabric buyers. You bring the idea. They handle the rest.

  • Downtown LA location — you get face-to-face fit sessions for tight-fitting leggings and sports bras. Being close to your manufacturer matters, especially for styles that need precise fitting. Most founders don't see this until it's too late.

Contact: leftyprodco.com | (323) 515-9266 | 318 W 9th St, Suite 1010, Los Angeles, CA 90015

How to Read This Table

Three numbers shape your choice: MOQ, lead time, and production location.

  • Need Made in USA? Go with Precision Sportswear, Apparel Production Inc., LA Apparel, Royal Apparel, or Lefty Production Co. These five focus on domestic production first.

  • Tight budget, small first run? Precision starts at 24 pcs. Affix starts at around 50 pcs. Both keep your entry point low — no pressure to commit to large orders.

  • Scaling fast? Tri-Star runs 300–500+ MOQ with a 3–5 week domestic turnaround. That setup fits replenishment orders, not early-stage testing.

  • Need full design support? Tack Apparel, Lefty, and Zega all start from rough concepts. You don't need a tech pack to get going.

The right manufacturer isn't the most popular one. It's the one that fits your volume, your timeline, and your brand position — at the stage your business is at right now.

How to Choose the Right Custom Athletic Clothing Manufacturer for Your Brand

The manufacturer you choose isn't just a vendor. It's the single biggest variable in whether your brand succeeds or fails.

Wrong pick? You're stuck with 1,000 units of see-through leggings, a 90-day delay, and a supplier who won't answer emails. Right pick? You get consistent quality, predictable lead times, and a production partner that grows with you.

Here's how to think about it — based on where your business is right now.

Match Your Stage to Your Manufacturer

Early-stage brands (0–2 years): Your one job is to validate the product without going broke. Find a custom workout clothes manufacturer that accepts 50–200 pieces per style. They should offer full-package support — pattern, sampling, and production under one roof. First order turnaround should be under 60 days. Sampling costs should run $50–$150 per style. Any serious manufacturer will credit that back against your bulk order.

Growth-stage brands: Now you're scaling a proven seller. Your questions shift. Can they handle 500–3,000 units per style? Do they have experience with flatlock seams, four-way stretch fabrics, and compression fit? Can they hold color accuracy within ΔE<2 across runs?

Scaling brands: Volume is your leverage. At 10,000+ units per style, your negotiation point is FOB unit cost. Basic compression leggings from established Asian facilities benchmark at $5–$8 per piece. You also need ISO 9001, BSCI, or OEKO-TEX certifications to enter wholesale or retail channels.

Four Steps Before You Commit

1. Sample hard — measure everything.
Order two types: a stock sample to judge their baseline quality, and a custom development sample built from your specs. Measure 5–7 key points across three sizes. Acceptable deviation for fitness apparel manufacturing : ±0.5–1 cm. Anything beyond that is a red flag at bulk scale.

Also pull on the fabric. Target 15–25% spandex content for leggings. Stretch it 20–30%, hold for 30 seconds, then release. No clean snap-back means your customers will feel that after their third workout.

2. Run a Tech Pack communication test.
Send your tech pack and watch what happens in the next 48–72 hours. A strong cut and sew activewear manufacturer comes back with a detailed question list — fabric clarifications, stitch type confirmation, tolerance questions. A weak one replies with "OK, no problem." That single response has cost brands thousands in rework.

3. Verify production claims — don't take their word for it.
Ask for a video walkthrough of the full facility: cutting room, sewing floor, QC station, packing area. Check the equipment too — look for four-needle six-thread machines and flatlock machines. These are standard for custom performance sportswear USA production. No footage means keep looking.

4. Watch for the three most common traps.
- Fake "Made in USA" claims: Real domestic production means manufacturing in the US — not just final packaging. Demand a production address and cross-check it.
- Hidden MOQ structures: "MOQ 50" often means 50 per color, per size. That's 500+ units before you catch it. Get every MOQ in writing — per style, per color, per size.
- Fabric substitution: A factory that quoted 260 gsm legging fabric can ship 210 gsm without telling you. Run a third-party GSM and fiber composition test before accepting bulk delivery.

The right custom gym wear manufacturer matches your volume, timeline, and quality standard at your current stage — not your future one. Run the process above. Sample in parallel with three to five suppliers. Let the evidence decide.

Conclusion

Picking the right custom athletic clothing manufacturer in the USA is more than a sourcing decision — it shapes your brand.

These 12 manufacturers cover every major need. Low MOQ startups, sustainable production, performance-grade fabrics, team uniforms, full-package development — it's all here. Launching your first fitness line? You get tighter quality control and faster turnaround. Scaling an established brand? A domestic partner gives you a supply chain you can trust.

Here's what matters most: don't chase the cheapest option. Chase the right fit . That means MOQ alignment, solid production expertise, and a partner who gets where your brand is going.

Now it's time to act. Pick two or three manufacturers from this list. Request samples. Start those conversations today. Every successful athletic brand began with that first email.

Your brand deserves a manufacturer that works as hard as your customers do.
target_keywords: cut and sew athletic apparel USA

seo.title_tag: Top 12 Custom Athletic Clothing Manufacturers in the USA
seo.meta_description: Discover the top 12 custom athletic clothing manufacturers in the USA — ranked by MOQ, quality, and turnaround. Find the right supplier for your fitness brand.

cta_blocks.body: Get full-package development — from tech packs to bulk production — with low MOQs and fast turnaround. Tack Apparel works with startups and scaling brands alike.
Tell us your MOQ, budget, and timeline — we'll help you figure out the right production path for your custom activewear line.
Whether you're starting from scratch or scaling a private label line, Tack Apparel offers end-to-end custom athletic wear production made for brands that want to grow.
